content.problemStatement:
Consider an array A={a1,a2,...,an}A = \{a_1, a_2,...,a_n\}A={a1​,a2​,...,an​} of size n≥1n \geq 1n≥1. If ak=2x−1a_k = 2^x -1ak​=2x−1 for all 1≤k≤n 1\leq k \leq n1≤k≤n,  for some integer xxx (1≤x≤60)(1 \leq x \leq 60)(1≤x≤60), then AAA is called interestinginterestinginteresting arrayarrayarray.

You are given a number CCC. Find any interestinginterestinginteresting arrayarrayarray AAA of minimum possible size such that a1⨁a2⨁...⨁an=C a_1 \bigoplus a_2 \bigoplus...\bigoplus a_n = Ca1​⨁a2​⨁...⨁an​=C, where ⨁\bigoplus⨁ represents bitwise XOR operation.

If there exists multiple such arrays, output any one.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains single integer CCC on a single line.


Output Format

For each testcase, on the first line output the minimum possible size of array AAA satisfying the given property.
On the next line output the elements of array AAA in any order.
constraints: It is guaranteed in the input that sum of lengths of arrays over all testcases does not exceed 10610^6106.
